parameters.content:
## Survey Response Analyser

**Setup:**
1. Create YOUR_OPENAI_CREDENTIAL and YOUR_SMTP_CREDENTIAL
2. Set SURVEY_EMAIL_TO
3. Feed survey responses via webhook or use embedded sample
4. LLM identifies themes, sentiment, and top insights from open-text responses
5. Approval gate before insights are shared

parameters.jsCode:
const responses = [
  { id: 'R001', nps_score: 9, open_text: 'Love the speed and clean UI. The only thing I miss is a Gantt view for longer projects.' },
  { id: 'R002', nps_score: 3, open_text: 'The onboarding is confusing. I spent two days trying to understand how to set up projects correctly and gave up twice.' },
  { id: 'R003', nps_score: 8, open_text: 'Really good tool for solo work. Gets harder when your team has 10+ people — too many notifications and no way to silence specific projects.' },
  { id: 'R004', nps_score: 7, open_text: 'Works well for most things. I wish the mobile app was better — it feels like an afterthought compared to the web version.' },
  { id: 'R005', nps_score: 10, open_text: 'Best project tool I have used in 10 years. The recurring tasks feature saved us probably 2 hours per week.' },
  { id: 'R006', nps_score: 4, open_text: 'We signed up as a team of 15 and about half the people still use a spreadsheet. The learning curve is too steep for non-technical folks.' },
  { id: 'R007', nps_score: 8, open_text: 'Love it but please add Microsoft Teams integration. Our company is all-in on Teams and Slack is not an option.' },
  { id: 'R008', nps_score: 6, open_text: 'Good features but performance is an issue — the dashboard takes about 4 seconds to load when I have many projects.' },
  { id: 'R009', nps_score: 9, open_text: 'Switched from Asana and have not looked back. The recurring tasks alone paid for the subscription.' },
  { id: 'R010', nps_score: 5, open_text: 'I like the concept but the CSV export does not include all fields. We have to manually copy some things over to our reports.' },
  { id: 'R011', nps_score: 7, open_text: 'Solid product. Would love bulk editing of tasks — right now you have to update one by one which is painful for large projects.' },
  { id: 'R012', nps_score: 2, open_text: 'The search function is terrible. I cannot find tasks from 3 months ago without scrolling through everything.' },
  { id: 'R013', nps_score: 9, open_text: 'Excellent support team. Had an issue and it was resolved within an hour. The product itself is intuitive once you get it.' },
  { id: 'R014', nps_score: 6, open_text: 'Good tool but the pricing is too high for small teams. Would love a cheaper tier for 2-5 person startups.' },
  { id: 'R015', nps_score: 8, open_text: 'The task dependencies feature is underrated. Once we set those up, our team stopped missing handoffs.' }
];
return [{ json: { responses, survey_title: 'Product NPS Survey — Q2 2026', question: 'What is the main thing you would like us to improve or add?' } }];

const { analysis: a, nps_stats: n, meta: m } = $input.first().json;
const themeLines = (a.themes||[]).map(t =>
  `  [${t.sentiment.toUpperCase()}] ${t.theme} (${t.frequency} mentions)${t.actionable?' [ACTIONABLE]':''}\n  Quotes: ${(t.representative_quotes||[]).map(q=>JSON.stringify(q)).join(', ')}`
).join('\n');
const report = [
  `SURVEY ANALYSIS: ${m.survey_title}`,
  `Generated: ${m.generated_at} | Responses: ${m.total}`,
  `NPS: ${n.nps} | Promoters: ${n.promoters} | Passives: ${n.passives} | Detractors: ${n.detractors}`,
  ``,
  `TOP 3 INSIGHTS:`,
  (a.top_3_insights||[]).map((i,idx)=>`  ${idx+1}. ${i}`).join('\n'),
  ``,
  `CRITICAL ISSUES (detractor drivers):`,
  (a.critical_issues||[]).map(i=>`  ✗ ${i}`).join('\n') || '  (none)',
  ``,
  `DELIGHT SIGNALS (protect these):`,
  (a.delight_signals||[]).map(i=>`  ✓ ${i}`).join('\n'),
  ``,
  `THEMES:`,
  themeLines,
  ``,
  `FEATURE REQUESTS:`,
  (a.feature_requests||[]).map(f=>`  [${f.frequency}x] ${f.request}`).join('\n'),
  ``,
  `RECOMMENDED ACTIONS:`,
  (a.recommended_actions||[]).map(ac=>`  [${ac.priority.toUpperCase()}] ${ac.action}\n    Rationale: ${ac.rationale}`).join('\n'),
].join('\n');
return [{ json: { ...($input.first().json), report_text: report } }];
